mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor demand and rising aggregate income, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate earnings, pushing S&P 500 forward returns higher.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS increase by at least 200,000, or Rank IC over PAYEMS month-over-month change is < 0.02 over any two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.25%, monetary policy is restrictive enough to compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 5.33% as of 2023-09-01 is above 5.25% threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates at cycle highs
failure_condition: 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.25%, or the mean return under this condition is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation and a high fed funds rate create a toxic mix where the Fed cannot ease despite slowing growth, compressing valuations and reducing equity risk appetite, leading to negative short-term returns.
regime_hint: stagflationary or late-cycle high inflation regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the trigger events, or the combination of high inflation and high rates does not produce a statistically significant negative mean return (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4% in a late-cycle environment with persistent inflation signals an overheated labor market that forces the Fed to maintain or tighten policy, which suppresses equity returns as recession risks rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% in September 2023 is below 4%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment below NAIRU and inflation above target
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations in this regime, or the negative mean return under this regime is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units indicate strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which leads to higher GDP growth and corporate earnings, boosting the S&P 500 over the next quarter.
regime_hint: expansionary phase with robust housing demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when HOUST > 1500, or the mean return is not significantly above the unconditional mean (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and supply capacity, which supports corporate profits and leads to positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of weeks when INDPRO month-over-month change is positive, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns is below 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP and retail sales are growing strongly, the economy is in a demand-driven expansion that boosts corporate revenues and earnings, lifting the S&P 500 over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of quarters when both conditions hold, or the joint signal does not outperform a single-proxy model in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a deteriorating labor market that precedes consumer spending declines and economic contraction, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE spikes by more than 0.3 pp in three months, or the mean return under the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
